title: UTC-based minimum appointment date can reject or allow the wrong local date
reasoning: The minimum date for appointment_date is derived from toISOString(), which uses UTC rather than the user's local calendar date. Around midnight, users in time zones ahead of UTC can be allowed to pick their local yesterday, while users behind UTC can be prevented from picking their local today. Date inputs are local-date controls, so the min value should be computed as a local YYYY-MM-DD string.
reproduction: In a timezone ahead of UTC, open the form just after local midnight while UTC is still the previous day; the min attribute is set to yesterday's date. In a timezone behind UTC, open it late in the evening after UTC has advanced to the next day; today's local date is blocked.
recommendation: Format the current local date without using toISOString, for example by constructing YYYY-MM-DD from getFullYear(), getMonth()+1, and getDate(), or by using a date utility that preserves local calendar semantics.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for date field attributes or timezone boundary behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a test that freezes time near a timezone boundary and verifies appointment_date min equals the local date, not the UTC date.

history.reasoning: The original evidence file still exists, but the cited appointment date input has moved to src/pages/steps/Step3AppointmentDetails.jsx:171-177 and no longer uses new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]. It now builds the min value from getFullYear(), getMonth()+1, and getDate(), which matches the recommended local YYYY-MM-DD semantics for an HTML date input. A repository-wide search still finds toISOString().split('T')[0] in server routes and an unrelated DOB normalization path, but not on the Step3 appointment_date min attribute or another appointment date form min, so this specific frontend timezone-boundary finding is fixed. I could not run lint/tests because the sandbox policy rejected npm run lint; package.json also has no dedicated test script.
